title: boswellia pirottae
intro: Boswellia pirottae, also known as Indian frankincense or salai guggal, is a type of tree in the Burseraceae family native to India. The plant is prized for its resin, which contains boswellic acids, responsible for its medicinal properties. These properties include anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antioxidant effects, which contribute to various health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, improving arthritis symptoms, and promoting digestive health. Boswellia pirottae is commonly used in herbal preparations like capsules, teas, and topical creams, which can be found in Ayurvedic and traditional medicine stores.
